mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in 65% of triggered instances or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment rises during recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand that sustains corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform single-series baselines or produces negative returns when housing starts rise while consumption expenditures contract.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the index of industrial production reflect immediate supply-side bottlenecks that compress short-term equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: The signal shows no predictive accuracy during supply chain normalization phases or yields positive returns when industrial production dips below 105.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 220, inflationary pressures force markets to price in aggressive monetary tightening, which compresses equity multiples and produces negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally despite CPIAUCSL breaching 220, or the threshold fails to predict returns when inflation is driven by transitory supply shocks.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and a simultaneously rising unemployment rate indicates labor market slack and weakening productivity, forecasting negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The divergence pattern correlates with positive equity returns or loses statistical significance when payroll growth exceeds 200,000 monthly.

mechanism_narrative: Higher levels of real gross domestic product reflect stronger underlying economic activity that supports near-term corporate revenue expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over rolling windows or GDP growth fails to translate into positive short-term equity momentum during stagflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production during a disinflationary environment confirms resilient manufacturing output without triggering rate hikes, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The signal reverses to negative returns when industrial production rises alongside accelerating inflation or loses predictive power during financial stress episodes.
